_doc: Labeled retrieval cases for scripts/eval_retrieval.py, grouped by information-NEED. Each need has one target (expect relpath fragment, optional expect_heading) and MANY phrasings — to measure embed/paraphrase generality and whether enrichment lifts oblique wordings, not just the one phrasing that happens to share the note's words. Phrasing 1 is usually 'native' wording; later ones are deliberately vocabulary-shifted (the credentials-vs-tokens stressor). Seed with `crib import` first. See docs/retrieval-and-adoption.md §3, §5.2. (Flat {"cases":[...]} is still accepted for back-compat.)

queries: automatically inject memory into the prompt so the agent uses it without choosing to call lookup
push relevant notes into context instead of waiting for the model to query
stop the assistant reaching for grep before it checks stored knowledge
vocabulary gap credentials tokens reranking
a search for one word misses the note that uses a different word for the same thing
close the mismatch between query wording and document wording on the document side

combine semantic search with exact term matching
reciprocal rank fusion of two rankings
the reranker should fuse its order in rather than replace the result list
a cross-encoder precision pass that can promote but not overrule the hybrid result
blend a second-stage scorer in as a third voter
capture a conversation into memory automatically via a proxy or the chat logs
ingest a chat session either from its transcript file or by intercepting traffic
two ways to feed an automatic session summary: read the log or watch the wire

keep machine-written notes from outranking curated ones until a human approves
a low-trust staging area for unreviewed memory

per-write version ring for cheap rollback of a bad write
undo an automatic note edit by restoring a previous copy
keep recent revisions of a note so a wrong write is recoverable
